title: ISic000628. Epitaph of Abdalas
body:
Text encoding: Jonathan Prag; edition source: autopsy Autopsy by Prag, 2026-02-05, in Ramacca Museo Civico
1D(is) · m(anibus) · s(acrum) ·
2Abdalas · Domi-
3tiae · Domitiani
4magister · magnus
5ovium qui · bene · vix(it)
6in officio · ann(os) · LXXX

Text encoding: Jonathan Prag; edition source: autopsy Autopsy by Prag, 2026-02-05, in Ramacca Museo Civico
1D·M·S·
2ABDALAS·DOMI
3TIAE·DOMITIANI
4MAGISTER·MAGNUS
5OVIUMQUI·BENE·VIX
6INOFFICIO·ANN·LXXX

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

  • Text from autopsy (and identical to that of Salmeri except that he omits the interpunct after 'qui' in line 5)

English translation

Jonathan Prag

Sacred to the spirits of the dead. Abdalas (slave) of Domitia (wife) of Domitian, chief overseer of the sheep, who lived well in his position for eighty years.

commentary

This inscription, together with a very similar funerary inscription for Chius, magnus magister pecoris found some 16km to the northwest (ISic001693), offers clear evidence for the existence of large pastoral estates in this part of the island in the early imperial period (supported by the extensive archaeology for rural settlements and estates in the area). As is often noted, both Agrippa and the family of Sextus Pompeius owned extensive estates on the island from at least the time of the civil wars, and these in turn passed to the imperial family, which also fits well with this inscription (see, e.g. Horace, Odes 1.29, and Epist. 1.12; Ovid, Pont. 4.15.15). See the full discussion in Salmeri 1984, and M. Kajava, RPAA, 78, 2005-2006, p. 527-541 for a possible connection with ISic003154.

bibliography

  • 1985.0483 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001283
  • Salmeri 1984 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001608
  • Manganaro 1988 17 n.62 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001427
  • Wilson 1990 215 n.102 fig.175 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001509

Letters of moderate quality, with simpe serfis, and a fairly wide module. T is consistently overheight.

Line 1 35-37 Line 2 29-33 Line 3 28-31 (T is 34) Line 4 23-27 (T is 29) Line 5 24-27 Line 6 22-25 Interlineation line 1 to 2 12-13 Interlineation line 2 to 3 7-11 Interlineation line 3 to 4 4-10 Interlineation line 4 to 5 5-10 Interlineation line 5 to 6 8-9
